summary: Synarthroses include sutured bones connected directly through collagen fibers allowing no significant motion; Syndesmotic articulations where two long bone ends fuse using a band-like interosseous membrane also fall within non-movable joint type classification along with Gomphoses which describe strong bony attachment between teeth roots and jawbone socket walls held tightly together by specialized connective tissue structures ensuring stability but prohibiting flexibility or displacement during normal function cycles thus fitting perfectly among other listed choices
